units.title: Data Types, Operators, Variables, Conditional Statements
Loops, Arrays, Strings, User Defined Data Types
Structures, Unions, Memory Management, File Handling
Atomic and Molecular Structure
topics: Molecular orbitals of butadiene and benzene
Energy level diagrams for transition metal ions

units.title: Use of Free Energy in Chemical Equilibria
topics: Estimations of entropy and free energy

units.title: Periodic Properties
topics: Variation of s, p, d orbital energies

courseOutcomes.description: Understand the multidisciplinary nature and fundamental concepts of the environment.
Identify major types of natural resources, biodiversity, and the importance of their conservation.
Analyze various forms of environmental pollution and explore suitable control measures.
Understand and apply concepts related to sustainable development, EIA, and environmental legislation.
Demonstrate awareness of global environmental issues, ethics, and the role of technology in monitoring.

referenceBooks: Erach Bharucha, Textbook of Environmental Studies for Undergraduate Courses, Universities Press.
Benny Joseph, Environmental Studies, Tata McGraw Hill.
Subrat Roy, Environmental Science (AICTE Prescribed Textbook), Khanna Publishers.
Anubha Kaushik and C.P. Kaushik, Environmental Studies, New Age International.
teachingStrategies: Interactive lectures and real-world case studies.
Group discussions and debates on environmental ethics.
assessmentMethods: Internal Assessment: 40 Marks (Assignments, Case Studies, Class Tests, Attendance).

units.title: Multidisciplinary Nature of Environmental Studies & Natural Resources
topics: Definition, scope and importance of environmental studies
Forest, water, mineral, food, energy, land resources

units.title: Ecosystems and Biodiversity
topics: Food chains, food webs and ecological pyramids
Threats to biodiversity and conservation strategies

units.title: Environmental Pollution
topics: Air, water, soil and noise pollution
Role of individuals in pollution control
Public health aspects and disaster management basics

units.title: Social Issues, Sustainable Development & Environmental Legislation
topics: Climate change, global warming and ozone depletion
Environmental Protection Act, Water Act, Air Act
Role of NGOs and public awareness campaigns

units.title: Environmental Impact Assessment and Role of IT
topics: EIA process: screening, scoping, data collection and evaluation
Environmental ethics and green campus initiatives
Use of information technology and GIS in environmental monitoring
